claimText: Vikram3201 is India's first fully 'Make-in-India' 32-bit microprocessor.
evidenceExcerpt: India’s first 32-bit processor – Vikram3201... Fully ‘Make-in-India’ 32-bit microprocessor
examUse: Statement-based question on indigenous technology specifications.

claimText: Vikram3201 was designed and developed by the Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (ISRO) in collaboration with the Semiconductor Laboratory (SCL), Chandigarh.
evidenceExcerpt: Designed and developed by the Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre of ISRO in collaboration with the Semiconductor Laboratory (SCL) in Chandigarh.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the developing agencies.

claimText: Vikram3201 is qualified for the harsh environmental conditions of launch vehicles, serving as an advanced version of the 16-bit VIKRAM1601 used in ISRO's Avionics systems.
evidenceExcerpt: An advanced version of the indigenously designed 16-bit VIKRAM1601 microprocessor which has been flying in the Avionics system of ISRO’s launch vehicles. Fully ‘Make-in-India’ 32-bit microprocessor qualified for the harsh environmental conditions of launch vehicles.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific application domain of the processor.

claimText: KALPANA3201 is another 32-bit microprocessor developed by the Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre and Semiconductor Laboratory.
evidenceExcerpt: They have also developed KALPANA3201, another 32-bit microprocessor.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching KALPANA3201 to its type or developers.
